mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 5d returns in more than 60% of observations during early-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 250 trigger expectations of prolonged restrictive monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5d returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue baselines and earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through fundamental valuation expansion.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward equity returns in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, forcing equity market corrections and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO trends during tightening cycles produce positive 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, contradicting the supply-contraction premise.
